zh.lifeSummary: 當Albert I von Gorizia於1240年出生在奧地利格 武德 格拉迪斯卡時，他的父親Meinhard I Count of Gorizia-Tyrol40歲，母親Adelheid of Tyrol28歲。他去世於1304年4月1日在義大利卡拉布里亞雷吉 卡拉布里亞雷吉 迪 卡拉布里亞享年64歲。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Albert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Albert I von Gorizia was born in 1240, in Görz und Gradisca, Austria, his father, Meinhard I Count of Gorizia-Tyrol, was 40 and his mother, Adelheid of Tyrol, was 28. He died on 1 April 1304, in Reggio di Calabria, Reggio Calabria, Calabria, Italy, at the age of 64.
